empty.title: Markdown Viewer
empty.hint: Open a file or drop one here.
about.author: Created by Matthias Stumm in collaboration with Claude Code (Anthropic)
search.regexHelp.charset: any of the characters a, b, c
search.regexHelp.notCharset: none of the characters a, b, c
help.title: Help
help.feature.openFiles: Open Markdown files via dialog (Ctrl+O), drag-and-drop, “Open with” in Explorer, or the recent-files list.
help.feature.tabs: Multiple files in tabs side-by-side; up to two columns — tabs can be moved between columns by drag-and-drop or right-click menu.
help.feature.viewModes: Three views per tab: Source, Split, and Rendered. The chosen mode is remembered per file.
help.feature.sourceToggles: Optional word-wrap and line numbers in the source view — togglable per tab.
help.feature.search: Full-text search (Ctrl+F) with live matches, regular expressions (.* toggle) and case sensitivity (Aa). The question mark in the search bar shows a regex quick reference.
help.feature.autoReload: Changed files are reloaded automatically; deleted files are marked with a strikethrough in the tab.
help.feature.restoreSession: Session (tabs, columns, views) is saved on exit and restored on next launch — optional via the toolbar.
help.feature.links: Markdown links to other .md files open them in a new tab; http(s) and mailto links open in the system default; wiki-style links like [[Target]] are supported.
help.feature.theme: Light and dark theme — automatically follows the Windows system theme.
help.feature.languages: Interface in German, English, French, Spanish, and Italian — switch via the language menu in the toolbar.
help.feature.windowState: Window position, size, and maximized state are remembered on exit and restored on next launch.
help.feature.multiWindow: Move tabs to a new window: right-click a tab and choose “Move to new window” or “Copy to new window”. Multiple windows can be placed on different monitors; with session restore enabled, all open windows are reopened on the next launch.
help.shortcut.moveTab: Move active tab to the other column
help.shortcut.searchNavEnter: Next / previous match (in the search bar)
